assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends the 'Extended channel + no minor or node upgrades exclusion configuration' and provides gcloud commands combining both approaches

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: The response specifically recommends '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and includes '--add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support' with explanation that it auto-tracks version's End of Support

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: The response includes maintenance window configuration with '--maintenance-window-start "2026-01-01T02:00:00Z"' and '--maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA"' specifically setting Saturday windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: The response includes '--maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=90d' and explains it limits control plane patches to once every 90 days maximum, with configurable range from 24 hours to 90 days

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: The response recommends Extended channel and explains it provides 24 months of support vs. standard 14 months, with extra cost applying only during the extended support period (months 15-24)

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: The response warns against blocking all upgrades for extended periods, states it 'accumulates security debt' and is 'Not recommended for extended periods'

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: The response provides a multi-environment strategy section showing dev/staging/production cluster configurations and mentions the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency (90-day interval)

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response provides detailed gcloud commands including '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades', maintenance window configuration, and alternative exclusion configurations
